content:
===center

![atraxa](https://i.redd.it/2qpkyjaycu141.png)

*"Hear only harmony.  See only glory.  Speak only truth."*

*[[Veil of Assimilation]]*

===endcenter

===accordion
===panel:Introduction

## Introduction

It begins with the smallest drop of the glistening oil.  The oil is a mutagen that first twists the minds of the infected and can induce memory loss and personality changes. The oil eventually replaces all vital liquids in the host body, which prepares it for compleation, a surgical process that swaps the body's organic matter with artificial machinery.

The glory of Phyrexia will be brought to all.  They will be evolved, perfected, compleat.

This is a deck designed to give each of our opponents the faintest vision of a perfect Phyrexia.  A single thought, a single idea, a single drop of the glistening oil.  We then let the contagion spread, and watch as the onset of Phyresis sends them into a frantic race against the ticking clock of compleation.

Sometimes, the fear of Atraxa is more powerful than Atraxa herself.  This deck is designed to win without ever having her leave the command zone.

===endpanel
===panel:Pros, Cons & Power Level

## Pros, Cons & Power Level

✅ **Pros**

- Where other Atraxa decks live and die by their commander, this one has been designed to win entirely without her.  So if she sticks around, great!  If not, we're not dead in the water.

- This deck got a huge boost recently with the release of Phyrexia: All Will Be One and it feels great to pilot.

❌ **Cons**

- Infect/Poison isn't very well liked so you may not be able to play this deck in certain playgroups (though in my experience most people have been A-OK with it).

- A low-curve 4-color deck such as this wants good mana fixing and lands that come into play untapped, which, unfortunately don't come cheap.

☢️ **Power Level**

- This is a high-power casual deck.

- While it doesn't have combos, it can spread poison counters with brutal efficiency to knock opponents out very quickly.

===center

![atraxavanquish](https://i.imgur.com/RCiOljM.jpg)

*New Phyrexia's grandest design*

===endcenter

===endpanel
===panel:The Gameplan

## The Gameplan

Ok, let's address the elephant in the room - [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] is a removal magnet.  There's no getting around it.  If she hits the field, our opponents will attempt to get rid of her immediately.  As such, this deck has been constructed in such a way that it does not rely on her at all.  I've won games with this deck in which Atraxa never left the command zone.

How?  It's because we have hyper efficient ways of putting poison counters on all opponents and then using several effects other than Atraxa to proliferate.  If we can get her on board and have her stick around, that's great!  But we are not crippled if she doesn't stick.  Atraxa is an amplifier, not a crutch.  With that in mind, let's go over the overall gameplan:

**Start the Clock**

- The early-game goal is a blitz to apply at least one poison counter to every opponent.  The Proliferate ability allows us to choose any permanents and/or players with counters and give them an additional counter of each type they already have.  So obviously, to maximize the value of each Proliferate trigger, we want to have ALL opponents available to choose from when we proliferate.  But of course, if we have a chance for a free Proliferate and not all opponents are poisoned, then by all means go for it.

**Commence Phyresis**

- Once all opponents have a poison counter, we use our Proliferate effects as often as possible to give them more poison counters.  While that's happening, don't sit idly by!  Keep up the pressure with our evasive, infectious and toxic creatures.  Focus down the most threatening player and get them to 10 poison to knock them out, or spread the love (depends on how you want to play).

**Taste their Fear**

- Opponents will be so scared of Atraxa that they'll hold removal in anticipation of when she hits the field, but the reality is that we don't even need her.  If we have nothing else to do, we can cast her and get a Proliferate trigger at the end of turn but she's not essential to the gameplan.

===center

![atraxa2](https://i.redd.it/o5067hg11nba1.jpg)

*All Will Be One*

===endcenter

===endpanel
===panel:Packages

## Packages

===accordion
===panel:Ramp (15)

- [[Birds of Paradise]], [[Elves of Deep Shadow]], [[Noble Hierarch]], [[Biophagus]], [[Plague Myr]], [[Sol Ring]], [[Arcane Signet]], [[Talisman of Curiosity]], [[Talisman of Resilience]] and [[Talisman of Unity]] all produce mana on their own.

- [[Farseek]], [[Rampant Growth]], [[Nature's Lore]] and [[Three Visits]] all get lands out of our deck and onto the field.

- [[Herald's Horn]] makes our Phyrexian creatures cost [[symbol:1]] less to cast.

===endpanel
===panel:Removal (10)

- [[Vraska, Betrayal's Sting]] can turn a creature into a Treasure token which sort of functions as removal.

- [[Cankerbloom]] and [[Tear Asunder]] can get rid of artifacts/enchantments.

- [[Path to Exile]] and [[Swords to Plowshares]] can get rid of creatures.

- [[Assassin's Trophy]] and [[Anguished Unmaking]] can take care of any permanent, and [[Tear Asunder]] can do so if it's kicked.

- [[Damn]] can act as targeted removal or a board wipe if it's overloaded.

- [[Phyresis Outbreak]] can also function as a pseudo-boardwipe.

- [[Vraska's Fall]] can force opponents to sacrifice a creature.

===endpanel
===panel:Card Draw & Card Advantage (10)

- [[Vraska, Betrayal's Sting]] has a card draw effect on her 0 ability.

- [[Tamiyo, Field Researcher]] can draw us cards with her +1 ability (which synergizes nicely with so many of our evasive creatures) and with her ultimate ability.

- [[Guardian Project]] draws us cards for playing creatures.

- [[Ezuri, Stalker of Spheres]] draws us a card whenever we proliferate.

- [[Herald's Horn]] doesn't explicitly draw us cards but if we're lucky enough to reveal a Phyrexian on our upkeep, we get to put it in our hand so it can function as card draw.

- [[Ixhel, Scion of Atraxa]] can let us cast spells from our opponents' libraries, effectively functioning as card advantage.

- [[Contaminant Grafter]] can draw us a card if an opponent has 3 or more poison counters at end of turn.

- [[Infectious Inquiry]] is a one-time card draw effect that also applies widespread poison counters.

- [[Nurturing Peatland]] and [[Silent Clearing]] can be sacrificed to draw cards.

===endpanel
===panel:Infectious Toxicity (30)

- [[Vraska, Betrayal's Sting]]'s ultimate ability can give a player a total of 9 poison counters.

- [[Skrelv, Defector Mite]], [[Venerated Rotpriest]], [[Pestilent Syphoner]], [[Voidwing Hybrid]], [[Bloated Contaminator]], [[Flensing Raptor]], [[Tainted Observer]], [[Ixhel, Scion of Atraxa]], [[Necrogen Rotpriest]], [[Plague Nurse]], [[Contaminant Grafter]], [[Vishgraz, the Doomhive]] and [[Tyrranax Rex]] all have Toxic which applies poison counters whenever they connect with damage.  Several of these guys also have some form of evasion or ability that helps push damage through blockers which increases our efficiency at applying poison to opponents.

- [[Blighted Agent]], [[Plague Myr]], [[Plague Stinger]], [[Ichor Rats]], [[Phyrexian Crusader]], [[Whispering Specter]], [[Flesh-Eater Imp]], [[Viral Drake]], [[Skithiryx, the Blight Dragon]], [[Phyrexian Swarmlord]] and [[Inkmoth Nexus]] all have Infect which applies a number of poison counters equal to their power when they connect against an opponent.  Several of these also have some form of evasion to make it easier to give opponents poison counters.

- [[Swamp Mosquito]] is an older card that adds a poison counter to any player it deals damage to.

- [[Ichor Rats]], [[Vraska's Fall]], [[Infectious Inquiry]] and [[Phyresis Outbreak]] can give all opponents a poison counter on their own, making them the most efficient spells in our deck to get the contagion engine started.

===endpanel
===panel:Infectious Infiltrators (4*)

- [[Mother of Runes]], [[Giver of Runes]] and [[Skrelv, Defector Mite]] can all give a creature protection which allows us to swing in past blockers for easy poison counter application.

- [[Champion of Lambholt]] can essentially make our creatures unblockable if it gets big enough with +1/+1 counters.  The nice thing is that since we're playing a proliferate deck, we can speed up the process if she gets even one +1/+1 counter to start with.

- Although only four cards are mentioned here, recall that several of our creatures have some form of evasion (such as Flying) or way to push damage past blockers (Trample, Menace, etc.) so we actually have several ways to guarantee our opponents become infected.

===endpanel
===panel:Super Spreaders (14)

- [[Vraska, Betrayal's Sting]] lets us proliferate with her 0 ability.

- [[Metastatic Evangel]], [[Cankerbloom]], [[Thrummingbird]], [[Bloated Contaminator]], [[Evolution Sage]], [[Tainted Observer]], [[Ezuri, Stalker of Spheres]], [[Viral Drake]], [[Contaminant Grafter]], [[Karn's Bastion]] and [[Contagion Clasp]] all have proliferation abilities on them so we don't have to rely on [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] alone.

- [[Inexorable Tide]] lets us proliferate whenever we cast a spell, making it one of our most efficient proliferation engines.

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===center

![atraxa4](https://cdnb.artstation.com/p/assets/images/images/047/014/787/large/lenin-valerio-mini-version.jpg?1646561742)

*Submit to the next stage of evolution, submit to compleation*

===endcenter

===endpanel
===panel:Individual Cards Explained

## Individual Cards Explained

A lot of the cards in this deck are pretty self-explanatory.  *Reading the card explains the card.*  However, some do have some niche applications and synergies that aren't immediately apparent.  Take a glance through this section if any card in the list looks peculiar to you, otherwise feel free to collapse this panel and skip it.  You can collapse it by clicking/tapping the purple heading just above.

I also prefer to discuss my cards in order of ascending CMC to better visualize my curve.  If you're viewing this deck, I encourage you to click the "**Sort**" dropdown and select "**Mana Value**".

You can click/tap the heading of each category below to collapse it and make scrolling a little faster.

===accordion
===panel:Creatures

[[Elves of Deep Shadow]], [[Noble Hierarch]] and [[Birds of Paradise]] are all cheap, efficient mana dorks that can help us cast our more expensive spells just a little bit earlier.

[[Mother of Runes]] and [[Giver of Runes]] can tap to give another creature protection from certain colors, which is a great way to help our Infect/Toxic creatures get past enemy blockers OR this can be used defensively to save them from removal at instant speed.

[[Skrelv, Defector Mite]] does a similar job except it also grants the protected creature Toxic 1.  Note that this can be used on a creature without any form of Infect/Toxic to make it infectious (such as [[Elvish Mystic]]) OR it can be added to a creature who already has Infect/Toxic and make it apply an additional poison counter.  The point is, this part of Skrelv's ability never goes to waste and we can use it aggressively to apply more poison to whoever we hit.

[[Venerated Rotpriest]] has Toxic 1 so it can easily apply poison to any opponent without blockers but its real value comes from its ability to apply poison to any opponent whenever any of our creatures become the target of a spell.  So if they point targeted removal at our stuff, we can give them a poison counter in retaliation (or give it to someone else just to be mean).

[[Plague Myr]] is a simple mana dork that just so happens to also have Infect, so we can use this for mana or we can swing with it if an opponent has an empty board.

[[Blighted Agent]] has Infect and is unblockable, so it's a very easy creature to spread poison with.

[[Swamp Mosquito]], [[Pestilent Syphoner]] and [[Plague Stinger]] are just a cheap creatures with Flying and a way to apply poison.  You're going to see a lot of these in the deck because we want to be evasive and swing past any resistence to apply poison more easily.

[[Voidwing Hybrid]] does the same job except it has the additional ability to [[Regrowth]] itself from the graveyard whenever we proliferate, making it a pretty resilient creature in this deck since we have several ways to proliferate.

[[Slaughter Singer]] gives a little buff to each of our creatures with Toxic, making them just a bit more potent in combat.  While this doesn't apply more poison due to how Toxic works, it's still a little extra edge to help them out in case they do get blocked.

[[Cankerbloom]] has a variety of options we can choose from.  Most of the time we'll be saving it for the artifact/enchantment removal but if we want to be aggressive, we can use it to Proliferate instead.

[[Thrummingbird]] lets us Proliferate whenever it deals combat damage to a player and that's not difficult to do considering it has Flying.

[[Metastatic Evangel]] lets us Proliferate whenever we play a non-token creature, and with a creature count of 39 in the main deck, this will be a very easy way to proliferate.

[[Evolution Sage]] lets us Proliferate whenever we play a land, which works nicely with so many fetches we're playing in a 4-color deck.

[[Champion of Lambholt]] is mainly in here to help our infectious and toxic creatures get past enemy blockers.  Since she gets a +1/+1 counter each time we play another creature, she can grow quite quickly in a deck with a large creature lineup such as this.  And since we're proliferating so much, we can give her additional +1/+1 counters outside of her own effect.

[[Phyrexian Censor]] applies a [[Rule of Law]] effect but only on non-Phyrexian spells, so we can play multiple creatures per turn if we want and still cast one non-Phyrexian spell but our opponents will likely be restricted to just one spell cast per turn while this guy is out on the field.

[[Flensing Raptor]] has Toxic and Flying which makes it an efficient poison applier on its own but it has the added benefit of granting Flying to another Toxic creature when it ETBs.  We have a few that can take advantage of this such as [[Skrelv, Defector Mite]], [[Slaughter Singer]] and [[Necrogen Rotpriest]].

[[Whispering Specter]] is yet another creature with Flying and a way to apply poison.  This guy can also sac itself to force an opponent to discard cards equal to their poison count, a useful ability if we know somebody has tutored recently or if we just want to set someone back because they're too far ahead.

[[Tainted Observer]] is another one of these except this one also lets us pay [[symbol:2]] to Proliferate each time we play a creature.

[[Ichor Rats]] is one of our most efficient creatures since it immediately applies a poison counter to everyone when it hits the board, allowing us to begin the proliferation right away.  Beyond that, it has 2 power and Infect so it can continue to apply pressure even after it has triggered its ETB effect.

[[Phyrexian Crusader]] has Infect, 2 power and protection from White and Red so it can swing past certain creatures and easily apply 2 poison on every hit.

[[Bloated Contaminator]] is one of the best cards in the deck.  3 mana for a 4/4 is already pretty efficient, but it has Toxic and Trample so even if it becomes blocked it will apply poison to the opponent (if any of its damage goes through the blockers).  And finally, whenever it does deal combat damage, it lets us Proliferate.  This card has no downside.

[[Ezuri, Stalker of Spheres]] draws us a card each time we Proliferate, which is great in this deck but also particularly nice if we happen to have [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] on the field because it essentially means we draw a card on our end step.  Furthermore, we can pay an additional [[symbol:3]] when he ETBs to proliferate twice immediately and draw 2 cards.  So we can cast him for [[symbol:2]][[symbol:g]][[symbol:u]] OR we can (sort of) cast him for [[symbol:5]][[symbol:g]][[symbol:u]] to Proliferate twice and draw 2.

[[Flesh-Eater Imp]] has Flying and Infect for easy poison damage but it has the added benefit of being able to sac another creature at instant speed to give itself a +1/+1 buff for each one sacrificed.  This is a good option to have in case we just want to finish someone off, since we can sac creatures to buff the Imp and have it apply poison based on its power when it connects.

[[Viral Drake]] is yet another Flyer with Infect but this one has an activated ability that lets us proliferate, useful for if everyone has a poison counter and we have nothing else to spend our mana on (or if we're just satisfied with our board state and don't want to cast anything else).

[[Plague Nurse]] can give all creatures with Toxic another instance of Toxic 1.  Toxic stacks, so for example if we have [[Pestilent Syphoner]], activate [[Plague Nurse]] and connect with combat damage, the opponent receives poison counters equal to the total Toxic value on [[Pestilent Syphoner]].  In other words, Toxic 1 + another Toxic 1 for a total of 2 poison.

[[Necrogen Rotpriest]] makes it so that our creatures with Toxic apply an additional poison counter, just like that.  It can also grant deathtouch to one of them at instant speed, useful in combat if we're attacking or defensively if we want to deter attackers.

[[Ixhel, Scion of Atraxa]] has Flying, Toxic and Vigilance, making it a potent attacker but the real treat is its ability to exile and play cards from the top of our opponents libraries.  A very nice reward for spreading poison and gives us some fun stuff to play with while our opponents are on their way to Compleation.

[[Vishgraz, the Doomhive]] has Menace which makes it harder to block but it also brings 3 little Phyrexian Mites with it when it ETBs, just more ways to apply pressure to our opponents.  On top of that, it buffs itself with +1/+1 for each poison counter on our opponents.

[[Contaminant Grafter]] is basically a [[Bloated Contaminator]] on steroids, being a 5/5 with Trample and Toxic means it can easily apply poison even through blockers and lets us proliferate whenever any creature connects with combat damage so if we hit with multiple creatures in a single combat step, we get multiple proliferate triggers.  And finally, if even one opponent has at least 3 poison, we get to draw a card and put a land into play at the end of our turn.  Insanely good card in this deck.

[[Skithiryx, the Blight Dragon]] is just one of the best poison appliers in the deck, since it has Flying and Infect with 4 power, it'll apply 4 poison each time it hits.  We can also give it Haste if we have the excess mana to let it swing immediately and we can save it from destruction at instant speed with its regenerate ability.

[[Phyrexian Swarmlord]] creates little insect tokens with Infect on each of our upkeeps equal to the number of poison counters our opponents have in total.  That's a lot of pressure in the late game and it combos nicely with [[Champion of Lambholt]] because it'll give her several +1/+1 counters and make all of those little insects unblockable.

[[Tyrranax Rex]] is just a big, dumb Phyrexian Dino that applies 4 poison as long as any amount of its damage gets through blockers, and with 8 power that shouldn't be too difficult.  Furthermore, it has Ward [[symbol:4]] so any targeted removal pointed at is is going to cost significantly more if its controller wants it to resolve.

===endpanel
===panel:Planeswalkers

[[Tamiyo, Field Researcher]] has some really nice synergy in this deck.  Her +1 ability turns up to two creatures into a [[Shadowmage Infiltrator]] by drawing us a card whenever either of them deal combat damage to anything.  Most of the time, we'll be using this on our creatures since so many of them have Flying so they can easily get in for damage.  However in niche situations we can target enemy creatures as well.  We can also -2 her to tap down two permanents if there is something we're really scared of like [[Ulamog, the Infinite Gyre]].  And finally, if we manage to ult her, she gives us a permanent [[Omniscience]] for the remainder of the game which is huge advantage.  Keep in mind that as a planeswalker, she comes with loyalty counters and since we have so many ways to Proliferate, each of those proliferate triggers can be used to increase her loyalty, meaning reaching ultimate status is not that difficult with this deck.

[[Vraska, Betrayal's Sting]] can be cast for [[symbol:4]][[symbol:b]][[symbol:b]] for 6 loyalty or [[symbol:4]][[symbol:b]] and 2 life for 4 loyalty, so flexible casting depending on the situation.  Her 0 ability is an efficient way to draw a card and Proliferate, which not only works with our strategy but also gives her a Loyalty counter (this is her main way of gaining loyalty).  Her -2 is actually a pretty creative removal ability since it doesn't destroy the creature, it just turns it into a Treasure token.  And if we manage to ult her, she can immediately put any player up to 9 poison just like that.  We'll usually use this on the most threatening player and then just proliferate one time or hit them with Toxic 1 or Infect to finish them off.

===endpanel
===panel:Artifacts

[[Sol Ring]], [[Arcane Signet]], [[Talisman of Curiosity]], [[Talisman of Unity]] and [[Talisman of Resilience]] are all mana rocks that support our (relatively) high curve and variety of casting costs.

[[Contagion Clasp]] lets us pay [[symbol:4]] to proliferate which is a nice option to have if we have nothing else to spend our mana on OR if we just don't want to cast anything.  In some cases it's better to not over-extend and vomit our whole hand onto the field.  If all three opponents are infected, it might be better to just proliferate and chill so we have the resources to rebuild in case of removal or a boardwipe.

[[Herald's Horn]] is in here because we actually have 28 Phyrexian creatures in the main deck.  A lot of them received an errata long after they were printed so they are indeed Phyrexian even if it's not printed on their card (this mostly applies to creatures with Infect).  [[Herald's Horn]] lets us look at the top card of our deck each turn and if it's a creature with the declared type we can add it to our hand, essentially making it a free draw-a-card.  Furthermore, it makes our Phyrexians [[symbol:1]] less to cast so it functions as ongoing ramp.

===endpanel
===panel:Enchantments

[[Innkeeper's Talent]] is really good in this deck because it lets us put a free +1/+1 counter on a creature at the start of combat, which is great for Infect creatures so they can apply more poison when they connect.  Furthermore, once we get to the final upgrade, we double the number of counters we put on permanents AND players.

[[Guardian Project]] is just an efficient card draw engine in a singleton format such as EDH.  It essentially draws us a card whenever any non-token creature ETBs on our field.

[[Inexorable Tide]] is one of the best Proliferation engines in the deck, allowing us to proliferate on each spell we cast.  Once this is on the board, the Phyresis clock ticks much faster for each opponent with a poison counter.  However, I would recommend not casting this until each opponent is infected, since it is a removal magnet and we want to get as much value out of it as possible.

===endpanel
===panel:Instants

[[Swords to Plowshares]] and [[Path to Exile]] are just efficient, 1-mana removal spells that exile the target creature.  Getting around Indestructible effects for 1 mana is pretty good and instant speed makes them that much better.

[[Tear Asunder]] at its worst is just a strictly better [[Naturalize]], being able to exile any artifact or enchantment (again, getting around indestructible effects like [[Darksteel Forge]]).  But if we kick it for an additional [[symbol:1]][[symbol:b]], we can exile ANYTHING.  A super versatile removal spell.

[[Assassin's Trophy]] and [[Anguished Unmaking]] can deal with any permanent.  Not a whole lot to say here, just some more versatile targeted removal spells at instant speed.

===endpanel
===panel:Sorcery Spells

[[Rampant Growth]], [[Farseek]], [[Nature's Lore]] and [[Three Visits]] are just some cheap, efficient ramp spells that get lands out of our deck and onto the field.  Resilient ramp since lands are generally harder to remove than mana rocks and mana dorks, plus these spells thin the deck and are pretty essential in a 4-color deck with a variety of casting costs.

[[Damn]] can act as targeted removal if we just need to get rid of one problematic creature or we can overload it for [[symbol:b]][[symbol:b]][[symbol:w]][[symbol:w]] and turn it into a [[Damnation]] instead.  Just a versatile spell that can function as different types of removal.

[[Phyresis Outbreak]] is one of our efficient poison counter distributers since it immediately gives one to each opponent.  Furthermore, it then gives all of their creatures -1/-1 for each poison counter they have.  So in the early game this is a great way to start the contagion and proliferation, but in the late game it can function as a sort of [[Toxic Deluge]] for just our opponents' creatures!

[[Infectious Inquiry]] is just a great way to immediately give all opponents a poison counter and replace itself by drawing us two cards.

===endpanel
===panel:Lands

I'm not going to go over every land in the deck but I will explain the mana distribution and why I feel this land base is solid for this particular build.  Green is by far the most important color in the deck, since our early ramp and mana fixing spells mostly require green to cast, and they help us find our other colors.  For that reason, nearly every land either produces green or can fetch something that produces green.

Aside from that, there are a few other lands that are notable:

[[Path of Ancestry]], for similar reasons that [[Herald's Horn]] is in the deck, is pretty good in a Phyrexian tribal deck with 28 valid creatures that share a type with Atraxa.  Scrying each time we use mana from it to cast a Phyrexian is pretty good value over time.

[[Inkmoth Nexus]] can turn itself into a creature with Infect and Flying, so we can get that extra bit of poison damage if we have nothing else to spend our mana on.  This land can also produce colored mana if we have [[Yavimaya, Cradle of Growth]] and/or [[Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth]] on the field.

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===center

![atraxa3](https://www.hipstersofthecoast.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/2.Atraxa-Repaint.jpg)

*Augmented, perfected, compleat*

===endcenter
